title: Ledger money-arithmetic built on roundMoney (ADR 0003)
content: ai-bookkeeper ledger now has src/features/ledger/lib/arithmetic.ts: addMoney(a,b,currency)/subtractMoney(a,b,currency). Design that composes with the ADR 0002 stack: scale each operand to INTEGER minor units (Math.round(value*10**minorUnits)), combine in integer space, divide back, then pass the final value through roundMoney(result,currency) — so roundMoney stays the single source of truth for minor-unit/half-even rounding and -0 normalization. Integer round-trip is what makes 0.1+0.2===0.30 exactly. Operands accept a bare number OR a tagged Money {amount,currency}; a tagged operand with a non-matching currency is the only expressible cross-currency op and is rejected. Errors use MoneyArithmeticError extends RangeError with .code 'not-finite'|'mixed-currency' — extends RangeError so existing fail-loud catch sites keep working while gaining a discriminable cause. Negative subtraction results are valid (expense>income). Module does NOT enforce MAX_AMOUNT ($1M, ADR 0001) — bounding sums stays a caller/isValidSignedAmount concern. Open follow-ups now: (a) N-ary sumMoney reducer over addMoney, (b) ADR 0002 Task 4 converge roundToCents->roundMoney — both blocked on the first real aggregation consumer. Reviewer's canonical check = roundMoney(arithmeticResult) is idempotent.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 18:29 UTC
ADR 0003 handoff: verify+commit pattern held (Dev Clone, team mode)
content: Confirmed the recurring ai-bookkeeper team-mode pattern: the Architect persona shipped ADR 0003 (ledger money-arithmetic, src/features/ledger/lib/arithmetic.ts: addMoney/subtractMoney built on roundMoney via integer minor-unit arithmetic) but left it UNCOMMITTED — git status showed arithmetic.ts + arithmetic.test.ts untracked and index.ts modified. Dev Clone's correct value-add was verify+commit, NOT re-implement: ran npm test (34/34: 12 amount + 13 round + 9 arithmetic), tsc --noEmit clean, eslint clean, then committed ONLY the 4 scoped files (commit 300e220 on main). Scope-trap check this time PASSED: the barrel index.ts re-exports only already-tracked siblings (round/amount/ingest), so a fresh checkout still compiles — no cross-work-order integration gap to flag. Note: the pre-existing e2e TS2783 error mentioned in earlier runs did not surface this run (tsc fully clean). Push/merge-to-master left as a human review gate (architect/dev does not self-merge under review_each_pr).

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 18:32 UTC
ADR 0003 money-arithmetic reviewed APPROVE — half-up-at-scale vs half-even doc gap (ai-bookkeeper ledger)
content: Reviewed commit 300e220 (ADR 0003: addMoney/subtractMoney in src/features/ledger/lib/arithmetic.ts). Verdict APPROVE, no security/critical/correctness blockers. Build-integrity gate PASSED this run: every barrel (index.ts) re-exported sibling (amount/round/ingest) is git-tracked, so a clean checkout compiles — no untracked-sibling trap (contrast b29eeaa/ADR 0002). Substantive note (Low, out-of-domain): the module docstring claims roundMoney (half-even, ADR 0002) is 'the single source of truth for rounding,' but operand scaling uses Math.round (half-UP) BEFORE roundMoney sees the value (arithmetic.ts combine()). For sub-minor-unit ties this diverges: addMoney(2.675,0,'USD') yields 2.67, while roundMoney(2.675,'USD') yields 2.68 (half-even). Not an active bug — amount.ts enforces ≤2dp at the parse boundary so over-precise figures never reach the primitive; the idempotency test only checks roundMoney(r)===r, which holds. Fix options recorded: route raw operands through roundMoney before scaling, OR soften the docstring to 'operands are pre-rounded half-up to minor units.' Overflow safe: MAX_AMOUNT=1e6, max minor sum ~2e9 ≪ MAX_SAFE_INTEGER. Pattern to keep: this team's reviewer value-add = re-run the gate + barrel-tracking check, not re-implement.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 18:36 UTC
ai-bookkeeper postcss CVE STILL OPEN — overrides pin not landed, only top-level bumped
content: Re-scan of ai-bookkeeper at commit 300e220. The standing postcss XSS finding (GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93, affects postcss <8.5.10, moderate) is STILL OPEN — partial remediation only. The team added a direct devDependency postcss@^8.5.15 (package.json:36), which fixed the top-level/Tailwind chain, BUT next@16.2.6 hard-pins postcss@8.4.31 as its own dependency (package-lock.json:3442), installed nested at node_modules/next/node_modules/postcss@8.4.31 and untouched. npm audit confirms 2 moderate. The required fix — an `overrides` block ("postcss": ">=8.5.10") — has NOT landed; a direct devDep bump cannot reach a transitive pin. Ranked High per known-CVE policy; real reachability low (build-time CSS stringify). Next re-scan: check package.json for an overrides block / re-run npm audit before re-reporting. Rest of codebase remains clean: 0 secrets (only .env.example tracked), 0 risky patterns (no eval/exec/innerHTML/process.env, no app/api routes). New ADR 0003 arithmetic.ts is pure/fail-loud, no security surface; only Low note = no MAX_AMOUNT magnitude bound in combine() (data-integrity, not exploitable).

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 18:40 UTC
ai-bookkeeper v0.4.0 shipped; deferred v0.3.0 tag finally cut
content: Released ai-bookkeeper v0.4.0 on 2026-05-28 (MINOR, 0.3.0->0.4.0) for the ADR 0003 money-arithmetic increment (addMoney/subtractMoney, integer-minor-unit, fail-loud, 34/34 tests) + the 'Ruled Ledger' visual v2 identity + brand assets — all backward-compatible features, no breaking changes (git log v0.3.0..HEAD = 2 feat + 1 chore). KEY: v0.3.0 had been documented (CHANGELOG/package.json/release-notes) but NEVER TAGGED — its tag was deferred behind a release-integrity gate (barrel src/features/ledger/index.ts re-exported then-untracked amount.ts/ingest.ts). That gate is now CLEARED (those files committed at 3aaf2c9, arithmetic.ts at 300e220, all barrel files tracked, tsc clean, tests green), so I cut the deferred v0.3.0 annotated tag at 3aaf2c9 alongside v0.4.0 at release commit de4137b. Tag anchor lesson: v0.3.0 belongs at 3aaf2c9 (where version bumped + amount/ingest committed), NOT b29eeaa (rounding-only, gate not yet cleared) as the old v0.3.0.md guessed. Pushed main + both tags + published GitHub Releases on xkazm04/xprize-ai-bookkeeper. Carry-forward: postcss XSS GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 STILL OPEN via next@16.2.6 nested postcss@8.4.31 — documented as known issue, fix = package.json overrides, NOT blocking the release.
